compounds.name: Calcium Nitrate
compounds.notes: Most common Ca and NO3 source. Part A only. Dissolves readily.

compounds.name: Potassium Nitrate
compounds.notes: Clean K and NO3 source. Compatible with both A and B. Often used in Part B.

compounds.name: Monopotassium Phosphate (MKP)
compounds.notes: Primary P and additional K source. Part B only (incompatible with Ca).

compounds.name: Potassium Sulfate
compounds.notes: Low-chloride K and S source. Part B only (incompatible with Ca).

compounds.name: Magnesium Sulfate (Epsom Salt)
compounds.notes: Cheapest Mg source. Widely available. Part B.

compounds.name: Magnesium Nitrate
compounds.notes: Alternative Mg source when extra NO3 is needed. Part A.

compounds.name: Ammonium Nitrate
compounds.notes: Use sparingly — high NH4 can be toxic at elevated temps (>25°C). Part B.

compounds.name: Ammonium Sulfate
compounds.notes: Acidifying NH4 and S source. Part B.

compounds.name: Mono-ammonium Phosphate (MAP)
compounds.notes: P source with some NH4. Incompatible with Ca (Part B).

compounds.name: Dipotassium Phosphate (DKP)
compounds.notes: Higher pH P source. Part B.

compounds.name: Iron DTPA 10%
compounds.notes: Chelated iron. Stable pH 4-7. Sequestrene 330. Part A only.

compounds.name: Iron EDTA 13%
compounds.notes: Chelated iron. Stable up to pH 6.5. Part A only.

compounds.name: Iron EDDHA 6%
compounds.notes: Most stable chelated iron — works up to pH 9. Use for alkaline water. Part A.

compounds.name: Iron Sulfate
compounds.notes: Cheap but unchelated — precipitates easily above pH 6.5. Oxidizes quickly. Part B.

compounds.name: Manganese Sulfate
compounds.notes: Standard Mn source. Part B.

compounds.name: Zinc Sulfate
compounds.notes: Standard Zn source. Part B.

compounds.name: Copper Sulfate
compounds.notes: Standard Cu source. Use very small amounts — Cu toxicity is easy to reach. Part B.

compounds.name: Boric Acid
compounds.notes: Primary B source. Small amounts — B toxicity window is narrow. ⚠ Limited solubility (4.7 g/100mL) — dissolve in hot water first if using large amounts. Part B.

compounds.name: Sodium Molybdate
compounds.notes: Mo source. Tiny amounts needed (0.05-0.5 ppm). Adds trace Na. Part B.

compounds.name: Potassium Silicate
compounds.notes: Si and K source. Raises pH significantly — add to water FIRST, separately from other salts. Never mix dry with other compounds. Special handling.

compounds.name: Nickel Sulfate
compounds.notes: Ni source for RO/ultra-pure water systems. ALERT: Ni is typically present as trace contamination in tap water — only dose deliberately if using RO water or confirmed Ni deficiency. Typical target: 0.05 ppm. Ni activates urease (N metabolism, urea hydrolysis). 17th essential element confirmed 1987. Part B.

bugbee_standard.label: Bugbee Standard (USU)
bugbee_standard.notes: All-nitrate formula recommended by Dr Bruce Bugbee, Utah State. Nitrate-dominant N prevents ammonium toxicity at typical grow temps (>18°C). Ca:Mg ~4:1. Ref: Bugbee & Salisbury, Utah State Plant Science.

hoagland_2.label: Hoagland #2 (reference)
hoagland_2.notes: Classic Hoagland & Arnon No. 2 solution (1950). Universal research baseline — full-strength single salt formula. Most modern recipes are variations of this.

lettuce_seedling.label: Lettuce — Seedling
lettuce_seedling.notes: Low EC for germination and early seedling. High NO3:NH4 ratio (7:1). Scale up to Lettuce Grow as true leaves develop.

lettuce_grow.label: Lettuce — Grow
lettuce_grow.notes: Bugbee-aligned lettuce formula. High NO3:NH4 (14:1). Ca:Mg ~4:1. Ideal for NFT, DWC, aeroponic at pH 5.6-6.2. Raise EC 0.2 mS/cm per week after transplant.

tomato_veg.label: Tomato — Vegetative
tomato_veg.notes: Vegetative growth phase. Higher N for leaf/stem development. Moderate K. Shift to Bloom/Fruit when first flowers open.

tomato_bloom.label: Tomato — Bloom/Fruit
tomato_bloom.notes: High K:N ratio drives fruit fill and brix. Reduce N, raise K. Ca critical for blossom-end rot prevention. Match EC to VPD — higher EC = higher brix but slower growth.

cannabis_veg.label: Cannabis — Veg
cannabis_veg.notes: Cannabis vegetative phase. Higher N drives canopy development. Keep pH 5.8-6.2 in hydro. Watch for Ca deficiency under LED at high PPFD.

cannabis_bloom.label: Cannabis — Bloom
cannabis_bloom.notes: Transition to high K:N for resin production. Maintain Ca for cell wall integrity. Flush last 7-10 days with plain water (EC 0).

kratky_lettuce.label: Kratky — Lettuce (passive DWC)
kratky_lettuce.notes: Passive non-circulating hydroponics. Start at EC 1.0 for seedlings; top up with 1.4 solution as roots develop. pH tends to rise — check weekly.

strawberry_fruit.label: Strawberry — Fruiting
strawberry_fruit.notes: Strawberries are salt-sensitive — keep EC 1.2-2.0. Moderate K for flavour. High Ca critical for firmness. Avoid excess N during fruiting (reduces brix).

basil_herb.label: Basil / Herbs
basil_herb.notes: Low EC for most culinary herbs. Higher K improves essential oil content and flavour. Avoid excess N — dilutes flavour compounds in basil.

flush.label: Flush / Final Week
flush.notes: Plain water flush — clears residual salts before harvest. Maintain pH 6.0. Watch for deficiency symptoms if flushing for >5 days.
